name: Prose: "I Can't Climb Trees Anymore" — Ruskin Bond

sourceRefs.label: 2022_q page 7 Q19 (Ruskin Bond honoured with the Sahitya Akademi Award, with answer key — applies to any of his 3 syllabus pieces)
brainkart.com/englishsummary.com/learncram.com - plot summary corroborated across multiple independent sources

researchNotes: OCR grep for 'climb trees/ruskin bond' surfaced a general Ruskin Bond bio fact (Sahitya Akademi Award) but no plot-specific past-paper hit for THIS particular story — a dedicated TNPSC MCQ page for this exact title wasn't found despite searching (unlike his other 2 syllabus pieces, which do have dedicated pages — to revisit if a future search turns one up). Plot content below is corroborated consistently across multiple independent summary sources, so confidence is reasonably high despite lacking a direct past-paper hit.

reading.intro: A nostalgic short story about a middle-aged narrator who revisits his old childhood home — now owned by a Colonel — and reconnects with memories of his youth through the Colonel's daughter and a jackfruit tree he used to climb.

qa.question: Who wrote "I Can't Climb Trees Anymore", and what major literary honour has he received for his contribution to Indian literature?
qa.answer: Ruskin Bond — honoured with the Sahitya Akademi Award (confirmed by a real TNPSC exam question, which applies to Bond generally across his works).

qa.question: What is the central premise of the story — why does the narrator revisit his old house, and who does he meet there?
qa.answer: The narrator (an older man) revisits his childhood home, which is no longer owned by his family — it now belongs to a Colonel. There, he meets the Colonel's young daughter, and shares his childhood memories with her.

qa.question: Why was the jackfruit tree special to the narrator as a boy?
qa.answer: It was his favourite childhood spot — a thick branch stretched out over the roof, and there was a small hollow in it where he used to hide his treasures.

qa.question: What treasure does the narrator remember hiding in the tree, and what is its significance?
qa.answer: His grandfather's Iron Cross — a German military decoration awarded for bravery, which his grandfather obtained from a German soldier while fighting in France during World War I.

qa.question: Why can't the narrator retrieve the Iron Cross himself, and who does it instead?
qa.answer: He is now too old to climb the tree himself (hence the story's title) — the Colonel's daughter climbs the tree for him and retrieves the dusty Iron Cross.

qa.question: What does the narrator do with the Iron Cross once it's retrieved, and what does this reveal about the story's real theme?
qa.answer: He gives the Iron Cross to the girl as a gift, out of gratitude — revealing that what he truly came searching for wasn't the physical object, but a reconnection with his own lost youth. The story's central theme is nostalgia and the fleeting, irretrievable nature of childhood/youth.

questions.prompt: "I Can't Climb Trees Anymore" was written by:
Ruskin Bond was honoured with which award for his contribution to Indian literature?
When the narrator revisits his old childhood home in the story, who now owns it?
Which tree was the narrator's favourite childhood spot, with a hollow where he hid his treasures?
What treasure had the narrator hidden in the tree's hollow as a boy?
How did the narrator's grandfather obtain the Iron Cross?
options: He bought it at an auction
He obtained it from a German soldier while fighting in France in WWI
It was a family heirloom from his own father
He won it in a card game
questions.explanation: From a German soldier, fighting in France during WWI.

questions.prompt: Why couldn't the narrator retrieve the Iron Cross himself?
options: The tree had been cut down
He was now too old to climb the tree
He had forgotten where it was
questions.explanation: He was too old to climb the tree.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator do with the Iron Cross after the girl retrieved it for him?
options: Gave it to the girl as a gift
questions.explanation: Gave it to the girl as a gift.
